health_zone_table.basis: SitRep49 Table 2 (02 July 2026) visually reviewed against the INSP PDF (post 25085; SitRep_MVE_RDC_N049_02_07_2026 sha256 e47833cc259a49798d094acab04b660012a37d18008533486cfc96d0e604728c, 14 pages).

reconciliation.note: Rows promoted here reconcile to official province/national totals: 1485 named confirmed cases plus 17 Ituri cases awaiting ventilation = 1502; all 473 confirmed deaths are named-zone attributed. The residual row is retained as unventilated context and excluded from mappable named-zone attribution.

lab_indicators_24h.note: National aggregate from SitRep49 section 4.3: Ituri 100 collected/analyzed / 33 positive (33.0%); Nord-Kivu 94 collected/analyzed / 9 positive (9.6%); Sud-Kivu 2 collected/analyzed / 0 positive. Positivity uses 42 positives over 196 analyzed samples.

care_continuity: SitRep49 published a full patient-movement table (Tableau 6). End-of-day isolation stood at 628 patients at 89.4% global occupancy (beds J-1 644; 72 admissions; 88 exits: 16 deaths, 55 non-cases, 16 recovered, 1 escape). The confirmed/suspected split is a fresh reported split this cycle: 215 confirmed-in-isolation and 413 suspected-in-isolation. Nord-Kivu isolation runs at 136% occupancy (challenge table cites 138.8% saturation).
Continuity of care reports 102 confirmed people-deprived-of-liberty (PPL) cumulative, of whom 33 died (24.5%) and 36 recovered.
highlights: 42 new confirmed cases including 7 community deaths were reported for 2026-07-02, in Ituri (33 including 5 deaths) and Nord-Kivu (9 including 2 deaths).
The 42 new cases came from Rwampara (14), Bunia (10), Mongbwalu (3), Bambu (2), Mandima (2), Lita (1) and Tchomia (1) in Ituri and Butembo (4), Katwa (2), Beni (2) and Kyondo (1) in Nord-Kivu.
No new affected health zone was reported on 2026-07-02; affected zones remain 36/104 and Ituri stays at 24/36 zones touched. Sud-Kivu has recorded no confirmed case since 2026-05-26.

14 further deaths were recorded among previously confirmed CTE/CT patients (13 Ituri, 1 Nord-Kivu at CTE Katwa), bringing confirmed deaths to 473. 16 patients recovered (15 Ituri, 1 Nord-Kivu), bringing DRC cumulative recoveries to 229.
Mongbwalu carries a 49.8% case-fatality (136 deaths for 273 cases); Nord-Kivu remains the highest-lethality province at 56.4%, led by Katwa (64.2%) and Beni (60.7%).
laboratory: SitRep49 reports Ituri collected and analyzed 100 samples with 33 positive (33.0%).

Nord-Kivu collected and analyzed 94 samples with 9 positive (9.6%). Sud-Kivu collected and analyzed 2 samples, both negative. New diagnostic labs are being installed at Komanda, Nizi, Mambasa and Nia-Nia (Ituri).
publication_context: INSP SitRep 49 reports through 2026-07-02 and was published on the INSP WordPress site (post 25085; embedded PDF media 25086; WP publish 2026-07-03T20:10:19Z). The PDF title is SitRep N°049/MVB_02/07/2026.
SitRep49 supersedes SitRep48 directly (a single report-day advance); the cumulative rose by 42 confirmed and 21 deaths over 2026-07-02.
The outbreak is reported across 3 provinces and 36 of 104 health zones; no newly affected zone was reported this SitRep.
Ituri remains the epicenter with 1366 of 1502 DRC confirmed cases (90.9%) and 397 deaths (CFR 29.1%).
Nord-Kivu holds the highest provincial confirmed-case fatality ratio at 56.4%, led by Katwa (64.2%), Beni (60.7%) and Musienene/Kyondo (66.7%); 100% lethality observed in low-count Vuhovi and Lolwa.
redactions: Page contact details and operational names, if present outside table facts, are intentionally not promoted into public evidence text.
surveillance: 1078 alerts were reported and 767 investigated (Tableau 3), a 71.2% investigation rate (SitRep prose cites 759/70.4%); Ituri 31.1% (150/457), Nord-Kivu 100.0% (608/608), Sud-Kivu 69.2% (9/13).
213 suspected cases of the day were identified, including 63 deceased validated alerts.
11,360 contacts were under follow-up and 9,291 were seen, producing 81.8% follow-up coverage; Ituri 82.2%, Nord-Kivu 80.2%.

341 new contacts were listed (236 across 4 Ituri health zones and 105 across 3 Nord-Kivu health zones); 269 contacts completed 21-day follow-up (255 in Ituri, 14 in Nord-Kivu); 11 contacts became suspects (3 Ituri, 8 Nord-Kivu).

alerts_total.data_gap_note: Report (J-1) 108; new alerts 970; total alerts of the day 1078. Tableau 3 investigated sum = 767 (71.2%); the SitRep prose cites 759 investigated (70.4%) — an 8-alert internal variance, table values promoted. 341 new contacts listed; 269 completed 21-day follow-up.

challenges.finding: Community reluctance toward post-mortem sampling (EDS/swabs) and resistance delay confirmation, underestimate cases, and disrupt key activities.
challenges.response: Intensify RCCE, involve religious and community leaders, and reinforce team security.

challenges.finding: Insufficient CTE capacity with Nord-Kivu saturation cited at 138.8% in the challenge table; the banner reports national isolation occupancy 89.4% (628 patients), raising nosocomial-transmission risk.
challenges.response: Accelerate CTE construction/expansion and deploy additional temporary isolation structures.

challenges.finding: Contact follow-up performance reached 81.8% (challenge table cites 81.3%) versus the 95% target, leaving transmission chains uninterrupted.
challenges.response: Train, deploy, and motivate community relays (RECO) with reinforced supervision.

challenges.finding: Weak alert reporting in some affected health zones risks undetected, silent transmission.
challenges.response: Reinforce community-based surveillance and rapid alert investigation.

challenges.finding: Seventeen Ituri confirmed cases remain not yet ventilated by health zone, leaving gaps in transmission-chain reconstruction.
challenges.response: Conduct data reconciliation, prune duplicates, and reclassify/localize with active field verification.

challenges.finding: Insufficient supply of essential medicines (MEG) and medical inputs weakens holistic case management.
challenges.response: Advocacy and urgent procurement of essential medicines.

challenges.finding: Insufficient IPC inputs (PPE, chlorine) and limited training raise nosocomial-infection risk; the challenge table reports 96 health-worker infections and 19 deaths (CFR 19.7%).
challenges.response: Urgent procurement and reinforced IPC training.

challenges.finding: A backlog of un-analyzed samples in Nord-Kivu and late diagnosis persist amid a 54.2% provincial CFR (challenge table).
challenges.response: Deploy a laboratory catch-up plan and reinforce diagnostic capacity.

challenges.finding: Insufficient ambulances and isolation structures (a gap of about 20 centers) delay transfer and isolation and raise transmission risk.
challenges.response: Urgent deployment of logistics and additional infrastructure.

challenges.finding: Insufficient coordination and weak use of COUSP frameworks cause operational inefficiency and delayed decisions.
challenges.response: Reinforce coordination mechanisms at all levels and operationalize provincial COUSP.

challenges.finding: A financial-resource gap of about USD 20 million limits implementation across all response pillars.
challenges.response: Urgent resource mobilization and partner advocacy.

challenges.finding: Insecurity and limited access (CODECO, ADF) restrict operations and access to high-risk zones.
challenges.response: Strengthen security coordination and humanitarian access.

challenges.finding: Compromised continuity of essential (non-Ebola) health services raises indirect mortality.
challenges.response: Effectively implement free care and reinforce continuity of essential health services (CEHS).

challenges.finding: High population mobility raises the risk of rapid geographic expansion; this cycle 5 imported cases at Wamba (Haut-Uele) were counted within Nia-Nia, and a security incident was reported at PK 51 in Nia-Nia (Ituri).
challenges.response: Reinforce PoE/PoC surveillance and cross-border/inter-provincial coordination.

contacts_total.data_gap_note: 341 new contacts listed (236 in 4 Ituri HZs, 105 in 3 Nord-Kivu HZs); 269 completed 21-day follow-up (255 Ituri, 14 Nord-Kivu); 11 became suspects (3 Ituri, 8 Nord-Kivu). Sud-Kivu contacts remain exited. Challenge table cites follow-up at 81.3%.

patient_movement_total.data_gap_note: SitRep49 published a full patient-movement table (Tableau 6, CTE/CT/CI): beds J-1 644, admissions 72, exits 88 (16 deaths, 55 non-cases, 16 recovered, 1 escape), end-of-day isolation 628 at 89.4% occupancy. The confirmed/suspected split (215 confirmed / 413 suspected) is a FRESH reported split this cycle (not a carry-forward). Nord-Kivu occupancy stands at 136% (saturation 138.8% cited in the challenge table).

priorities.priority: Resolve the 17 unventilated Ituri confirmed cases without distributing them to named health zones; keep the Wamba/Haut-Uele imported cases counted within Nia-Nia

Relieve isolation load at 89.4% national occupancy and the Nord-Kivu 56.4% CFR; close the reported gap of about 20 isolation centers and accelerate CTE construction (new CTE site identified in Bunia; Nizi and Nyankunde sites evaluated)
Improve contact follow-up (81.8% vs 95% target), reinforce intervention teams and transport in affected ZS, and clear the Nord-Kivu laboratory backlog

preserved_conflicts: SitRep49 headline is DRC-only (1502 confirmed / 473 confirmed deaths across Ituri, Nord-Kivu, Sud-Kivu); the country-scope public snapshot adds the latest reviewed Uganda component separately.

The 1,502 confirmed total is marked re-actualized and subject to change after DHIS2 cleaning/reclassification/harmonization; SitRep49 supersedes SitRep48 directly (a single report-day advance), so treat the +42 confirmed and +21 confirmed deaths vs SitRep48 as an official one-day delta for 2026-07-02.
SitRep49 carries an explicit Ituri residual of 17 confirmed cases in zones not yet identified; these counts are public but not mappable to named health zones.
No new affected health zone was reported this cycle; the affected-zone count remains 36 of 104 (Ituri 24/36). No new map geometry was required.
The banner case-fatality is 31.5% (473/1502 = 31.5%; the SitRep narrative also cites 31.6%).

SitRep49 published a full patient-movement table (Tableau 6): end-of-day isolation 628 at 89.4% occupancy (down from 641 at 97.8% at SitRep48), with a FRESH confirmed/suspected split of 215 confirmed / 413 suspected (215 + 413 = 628). This is a freshly reported split, not a carried estimate. Nord-Kivu isolation occupancy is 136% (138.8% saturation cited in the challenge table).
Daily suspected cases (213) and the 63 deceased validated alerts are current-cycle validated alert outputs, not cumulative suspected stock or cumulative confirmed deaths.

Uganda anchor remains 20 cumulative confirmed cases (2 deaths, 11 recoveries) per the Uganda MoH EVD daily dashboard (last reviewed 2026-06-21); WHO AFRO Uganda External SitRep 07 (data as of 2026-06-28) independently reports no new confirmed Uganda cases over the past week. Carried forward this cycle as INSP reports DRC only, so country-scope is 1522 confirmed / 475 deaths / 240 recovered.

Sud-Kivu has recorded no new confirmed case since 2026-05-26, suggesting no recent transmission; it remains at 3 confirmed / 1 death across Miti-Murhesa, and its contacts remain exited from follow-up.
Several low-denominator zones report 100% case-fatality this cycle (Drodro 3/3, Kambala 2/2, Vuhovi 1/1, Lolwa 1/1); these are unstable small-number ratios.

The challenge table cites 96 health-worker infections with 19 deaths (CFR 19.7%), a Nord-Kivu CTE saturation of 138.8%, a Nord-Kivu laboratory backlog amid a 54.2% provincial CFR, an isolation-structure gap of about 20 centers, and a financing gap of about USD 20 million.

External response milestones reported outside the INSP SitRep are tracked as separate context in the LOVS narrative/synthesis, not in the INSP denominators: the WHO Emergency Use Listing of the first BDBV molecular RT-PCR diagnostic (2026-07-02) supports the SitRep-noted decentralization of diagnostic capacity (new labs at Komanda, Nizi, Mambasa, Nia-Nia); and the response financing picture (INSP-cited ~USD 20 million pillar-implementation gap; separately, an Africa CDC response requirement of ~USD 1.4 billion with pledges only ~13% released, a White House/OMB supplemental request of ~USD 1.4 billion still before Congress, and OCHA/UNDP macro-humanitarian and socioeconomic context) is recorded as distinct facets and not collapsed into a single figure.

review_notes: SitRep #049 (report date 2026-07-02; INSP WordPress post 25085 published 2026-07-03T20:10:19Z; embedded PDF media 25086). PDF sha256 e47833cc259a49798d094acab04b660012a37d18008533486cfc96d0e604728c, 14 pages. Title N°049/MVB_02/07/2026.

SitRep49 supersedes SitRep48 directly (single report-day). DRC headline 1502 confirmed / 473 confirmed deaths (CFR 31.5%); +42 confirmed and +21 confirmed deaths vs SitRep48. On 02 July, 42 new confirmed (Ituri 33, Nord-Kivu 9) carried 7 community deaths; a further 14 deaths occurred among previously confirmed CTE/CT patients (13 Ituri, 1 Nord-Kivu).
The 1,502 total is flagged re-actualized and subject to change after DHIS2 cleaning/reclassification; deltas are official revised deltas.
No new affected health zone this cycle. Affected-zone count remains 36 of 104; Ituri stays 24/36 touched. No new map geometry/centroid required.
Health-zone rows reconcile to 1502 confirmed / 473 deaths: 1485 named confirmed cases plus 17 Ituri cases awaiting ventilation, with all 473 confirmed deaths named-zone attributed.

Recovered advanced to 229 DRC cumulative (+16). Uganda anchor carried forward at 20 cumulative cases / 2 deaths / 11 recoveries (last reviewed 2026-06-21), so country-scope is 1522 confirmed / 475 deaths / 240 recovered.

Laboratory throughput promoted from section 4.3: Ituri 100 collected/analyzed, 33 positive (33.0%); Nord-Kivu 94 collected/analyzed, 9 positive (9.6%); Sud-Kivu 2 collected/analyzed, 0 positive (196 analyzed / 42 positive overall, 21.4%).

SitRep49 published a full patient-movement table (Tableau 6): end-of-day isolation 628 at 89.4% occupancy with a fresh confirmed/suspected split of 215/413. Operational data captured: alerts 1078 reported / 767 investigated (71.2%; prose 759/70.4%), 213 suspected cases of the day (63 deceased validated alerts), 11,360 contacts under follow-up (81.8%), 341 new contacts, 269 completed 21-day.

External context recorded separately (not in INSP denominators): WHO Emergency Use Listing of the first BDBV molecular diagnostic (2026-07-02) aligns with the SitRep-noted diagnostic decentralization; financing facets (INSP ~USD 20M pillar gap; Africa CDC ~USD 1.4B requirement / ~13% released; White House/OMB ~USD 1.4B request pending; OCHA/UNDP macro context) are kept as distinct facets.
